summary: Iteration 7 both-side testing: 18/18 pytests pass, Board Fill All Shortages + Persons page 1-based numbering verified end-to-end. Backend: POST /api/schedule/{date}/fill-shortages works — unfillable baseline (short=2) returns 200 with unchanged shortage/assigned; fillable scenario (cell cleared via /adjust) reduces total_shortage and re-populates the cell with a specialist-first free skilled candidate; results persist via GET; no person appears in 2 cells; 404 when schedule missing. Frontend: Persons page first row = '1 Aarsh Patel', last row = '76 Yuvraj Magharola', search filter renumbers to 1..N. Board /board?date=2026-07-16 shows critical banner + fill-shortages-btn when total_shortage=2; clicking it triggers API and shows info toast 'No free skilled staff available for remaining shortages' (unfillable case, shortage stays 2). After clearing P&C Assembly · X-Smart cell (creates 2 fillable shortages, total 4), clicking Fill All Shortages shows success toast 'Filled 2 of 4 shortages' and reduces shortage back to 2. Baseline restored at end.
critical_code_review_comments: fill_shortages endpoint correctly excludes absent + currently-used ids and prefers specialists via (total_skills, name).
Scarcity-first cell ordering guarantees the hardest cells get first pick of scarce candidates.
overrides append picks_existing + new picks so pre-existing assignments are preserved (won't drop extra hands).
unassigned.discard(cell_key) after filling — good, ensures previously-cleared cells become populated overrides.
One-person-per-day rule is enforced by `used` set updates inside the loop.
Persistence via the same generate_schedule flow means overrides are upserted; verified with GET.
PersonsPage now uses (idx+1) — correctly re-numbers on filter.
BoardPage computes `filled = summary.shortage - r.total_shortage` — matches success toast semantics (Filled N of M).
Minor: fill_shortages skips cells with zero eligible candidates silently — behavior spec-compliant but consider surfacing per-cell reasons in future for observability.
seed_data_creation: None; used existing standard config date=2026-07-16 shift=day (X-Smart p=1 rc=2, E2 p=2 rc=1, Spares p=3 rc=1). Baseline restored (req=40 ass=38 short=2) at end of testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py has 18 tests (12 regression + 6 iteration-7 for fill-shortages). Run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -o addopts= -v`. Standard baseline: date=2026-07-16 shift=day → req=40 ass=38 short=2. The 2 baseline shortages (E2 Trolley + Spares) are genuinely unfillable. To create a fillable scenario in UI: click any filled X-Smart cell → Unassign; then fill-shortages-btn refills it. TestZZZRestoreBaseline auto-restores at end of the pytest run.
